chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD CP No. D- 1659 & 1691 of 2019 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 05.04.2022 Mr. Imamuddin Otho, Advocate for Petitioners Mr. Ayaz Ali Rajpar, Asstt: A.G. The petitioners in CP No. D- 1659 of 2019 claim to have been appointed on daily wages basis with effect from 13.8.2012 as chowkidar and Malhi respectively; whereas in the connected petition, petitioner No.1 was appointed as Pesh Imam and Petitioners 2 and 3 as Malhis. As per letter dated 19.1.2021 of Assistant Secretary Board of Revenue, Sindh there is no record / data available of these alleged employees / petitioners. The letter was addressed to Deputy Commissioner, Matiari which is placed on record. Apart from this it appears that there were no codal formalities which were exhausted while the subject appointments were made. It appears to be fake and fabricated appointments. Be that as it may, now in the office of Deputy Commissioner Matiari the sanctioned vacancies are being filled on regular basis and the petitioners claim to have applied for the same posts, hence there is absolutely nothing on record to regularize the services of daily wagers whose record itself is doubtful. They may however are at liberty to participate in the ongoing process of appointment / recruitment on the terms and conditions as incorporated in the advertisement. Copy of the advertisement is taken on record. The petitions are dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE karar_hussain/PS*
